$schema: Diagram manifest for the ADL engineering-notebook SVG system
style: ADL engineering notebook: warm paper, ink linework, pencil construction lines, watercolor fills

phases.3A: foundational architecture, installation, and connectivity diagrams
phases.3B: troubleshooting flows and learning concept diagrams

diagrams.title: The ADL Linux stack
diagrams.description: Layered stack from Android up through Termux, proot, Ubuntu, XFCE, and applications.

diagrams.title: Desktop architecture
diagrams.description: Phone cutaway showing the software layers and USB-C video out to an external monitor.

diagrams.title: Where your files live
diagrams.description: Android shared storage, Termux home, and the Ubuntu container connected by shared folders and backups.

diagrams.title: Command process flow
diagrams.description: A command flows from the user through the shell and Linux environment down to Android resources.

diagrams.title: Hardware resource flow
diagrams.description: CPU, RAM, storage, battery, and display feed both Android and the Linux desktop session.

diagrams.title: Installation roadmap
diagrams.description: Seven steps from preparing Android to installing apps on the finished desktop.

diagrams.title: First launch sequence
diagrams.description: Open Termux, start Linux, start X11, open the desktop, see it on the monitor.

diagrams.title: Update and maintenance loop
diagrams.description: Check repos, download package lists, upgrade packages, clean unused files — repeat.

diagrams.title: Backup and restore flow
diagrams.description: The Linux container is archived to external storage or cloud and restored to any phone.

diagrams.title: Directory layout
diagrams.description: Termux home, Ubuntu root, and shared storage with downloads and backups.

diagrams.title: USB-C workstation workflow
diagrams.description: Phone to dock fan-out: monitor, keyboard and mouse, SSD, and power.

diagrams.title: Display output path
diagrams.description: Video path from phone over DisplayPort Alt Mode through the dock to the monitor.

diagrams.title: External storage flow
diagrams.description: Phone, dock, external SSD, and the Linux shared folder exchanging files.

diagrams.title: Bluetooth input flow
diagrams.description: Keyboard and mouse connect over Bluetooth to the phone and into the desktop session.

diagrams.title: Complete ADL workstation
diagrams.description: Desk scene with phone, dock, monitor, keyboard and mouse, and external SSD.

diagrams.title: Troubleshooting triage
diagrams.description: A problem fans out to five lanes: display, install, performance, permissions, packages.

diagrams.title: Display troubleshooting flow
diagrams.description: No display: check cable, dock, monitor input, desktop mode, then Termux:X11.

diagrams.title: Installation troubleshooting flow
diagrams.description: Command failed: check internet, update packages, check storage, retry, reinstall.

diagrams.title: Performance troubleshooting flow
diagrams.description: Slow desktop: close apps, check RAM, reduce effects, reboot, use lighter components.

diagrams.title: Permission troubleshooting flow
diagrams.description: Permission denied: grant storage, check Android app settings, rerun, verify access.

diagrams.title: Package troubleshooting flow
diagrams.description: Package error: update lists, fix broken packages, clear cache, retry install.

diagrams.title: What is Termux?
diagrams.description: Termux as the gateway between the Android world and Linux tools.

diagrams.title: What is proot?
diagrams.description: proot as a protected Linux workspace inside Android, no root required.

diagrams.title: What is a desktop environment?
diagrams.description: XFCE as the interface layer between the user and Linux applications.

diagrams.title: Linux components
diagrams.description: Kernel, userspace, shell, packages, desktop, apps, and your files.

diagrams.title: Application lifecycle
diagrams.description: Click app, desktop launcher, Linux process, window appears, user interacts.

diagrams.title: Desktop session lifecycle
diagrams.description: Start desktop, run apps, save work, stop session, return to Android.
